test_cases.input: A 1,200-word profile of a local politician including claims about their voting record, education credentials, campaign donation amounts, and a quote attributed to a political opponent.
test_cases.notes: All six verifiable categories were identified. High-risk flag was correctly applied to the opponent's attributed quote. Evidence requirements were specific and actionable.

test_cases.input: A science feature about microplastics in drinking water including statistics from three different studies, regulatory thresholds, and expert quotes. Short 800-word piece.
test_cases.notes: Correctly distinguished peer-reviewed studies from government data. Flagged that two statistics appeared to use different measurement methodologies, creating an apples-to-oranges comparison risk.

test_cases.input: A breaking news article about a corporate bankruptcy filing with specific debt figures, named creditors, and a characterization of executive misconduct based on a single anonymous source.
test_cases.notes: Anonymous source limitation was correctly flagged as high-risk without being overly cautious. Recommended court filing verification for all debt figures. Summary was accurate.
